mnemonics.title: INTERRUPT FLOW
mnemonics.content: CPU CHECKS: Interrupt Request → Controller Prioritizes → CPU Acknowledges → Vector Table Lookup → ISR Execution → IRET Returns State

mnemonics.title: IRQ PRIORITY
mnemonics.content: IRQ 0 (Timer) HIGHEST → IRQ 1 (Keyboard) → IRQ 2 (Cascade) → IRQ 3-7 LOWER PRIORITY IN PC ARCHITECTURE

mnemonics.title: INTERRUPT TYPES
mnemonics.content: MASKABLE: Can be ignored (INT) | NON-MASKABLE: Cannot be ignored (NMI) | SOFTWARE: Program-initiated (INT n)

mnemonics.title: SAVING STATE
mnemonics.content: SAVE: Program Counter + Status Register → JUMP to ISR → RESTORE on return (IRET instruction)

acronyms.usage: Hardware line used by devices to signal the CPU for attention
Special function that handles a specific interrupt event
Critical interrupt that cannot be disabled by CPU
Hardware managing multiple IRQ lines and priorities
Modern multi-core interrupt controller with local and I/O APICs
Signal from CPU acknowledging interrupt request
Signal indicating a device needs CPU attention

visualTips.visualization: Draw a flowchart: Device → IRQ Line → Interrupt Controller → CPU INTA → Vector Table → ISR → Return. Use arrows showing signal flow and decision points.
Visualize devices as circles in a series chain, each passing a token upward. The device closest to CPU that has the token handles the interrupt. Higher in chain = higher priority.
Draw a box with multiple input lines at bottom, showing how the highest input (1) propagates to produce binary output. Like a pyramid where only the tallest peak (highest priority) shows through.
Picture a stack growing downward: Bottom = old PC, then PSW (flags), then local variables. IRET pops in reverse order to restore exact state.

analogies.analogy: Just as a receptionist receives calls, prioritizes them, and notifies the appropriate person, an interrupt controller manages device requests, prioritizes them, and notifies the CPU.
Like a fire alarm triggering fire crews who respond quickly, handle the emergency, and return to standby, an ISR responds to hardware events and returns control to the main program.

analogies.concept: Priority Encoder as Talent Show Judge
analogies.analogy: Like judges picking the best performer from simultaneous auditions based on assigned priority, a priority encoder selects the highest priority interrupt from multiple simultaneous requests.
Just as emergency exits cannot be locked or ignored during fire, NMI cannot be masked and always gets immediate CPU attention for critical situations.
